title: CLI usage text doesn't mention plugin recipient/identity flags
body: The `usage` string in cmd/age/age.go documents `-r/-R/-i` for native and SSH recipients/identities, but doesn't mention how plugin-backed recipients (age1<plugin>...) or identities are specified on the command line. Running `age --help` gives no hint that plugins are supported at all.

title: scrypt work factor isn't configurable via the CLI
body: scrypt.go picks a fixed work factor for passphrase-based encryption (`age -p`). For a high-value archive I wanted a much higher work factor than the default to slow down offline brute-force attempts, but there's no flag to override it — is increasing the cost intentionally not exposed for a reason?

title: Unclear which SSH key types agessh supports as identities vs recipients only
body: agessh.go clearly supports ed25519 and rsa SSH keys in some capacity, but it's not obvious from the doc comments whether both types work as decryption identities, or if one of them (e.g. rsa) is only usable as an encryption recipient. A short compatibility table in the package docs would help.

title: Armor decoder doesn't cap input size before parsing
body: armor/armor.go's PEM-style decoder for "-----BEGIN AGE ENCRYPTED FILE-----" blocks doesn't appear to bound how much base64 it will buffer before hitting a proper END marker or failing. A crafted file with a huge unterminated armor block could make `age --decrypt` consume excessive memory before any actual decryption is attempted.

title: age hangs indefinitely if a plugin binary never responds on stdin
body: Using a third-party age plugin that has a bug where it stops reading/writing on its stdio pipes, `age -r age1<plugin>...` just hangs forever instead of erroring out after a reasonable timeout. plugin/plugin.go's client doesn't appear to have a deadline on the plugin's protocol handshake.
